name: Tour of the Font Roja Natural Park: loop from Alcoy
name_original: Vuelta al Parque Natural de la Font Roja — Circular desde Alcoy
description:
To the southwest of Alcoi is the Font Roja Natural Park, where oaks mix with pines and other tree species, giving the Mediterranean landscape a different touch. This circular route goes around this natural park from the Alcoi train station.

The itinerary takes you along several sections of greenway with different surfaces, both paved and gravel. Although the more than 47 kilometres and the nearly 700 metres of gradient are not large figures, on this day you do not pass through any towns, so it is advisable to bring enough food and water to complete the route. Even so, almost halfway along the route, you border Tibi, in case you feel like or need a detour.

Both sides of the Carrascar de la Font Roja are very different. The south is dry and desert-like, the north is wooded and with shady stretches. But on both sides, humans have taken advantage of the land to cultivate whatever is possible. Apart from the typical olive, almond and fruit trees, you can cross areas with cereal fields, which are much less typical of the Mediterranean interior. At the end, another stretch of greenway takes you back to Alcoi. If you didn't stop at the start, you still have time to enjoy this magnificent city full of history and culture.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Alcoi is characterized by its diverse terrain, encompassing two significant natural parks: Font Roja and Sierra de Mariola. The region features a mountainous backdrop with numerous bridges spanning the Riquer and Molinar rivers, creating a unique urban and natural landscape. Extensive greenways, repurposed from former railway tracks, offer dedicated cycling paths through varied environments. This area provides a range of gravel bike trails suitable for different skill levels, from moderate loops to challenging climbs. Best gravel bike trails around Alcoi The most popular gravel bike trail is Tour of the Font Roja Natural Park: loop from Alcoy, a difficult 29.3 miles (47.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 1 minute to complete. This route explores the dense Mediterranean mixed forest of Font Roja Natural Park. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Serpis Greenway and Beniarrés Reservoir: loop from Alcoy, a difficult 40.4 miles (65.0 km) path. This route follows the historic Serpis Greenway, offering views of the Beniarrés Reservoir and surrounding landscapes. Local gravel bikers also love the Sant Antoni Tunnel – L'Estepar Rest Area loop from Alcoi, a 22.6 miles (36.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Gravel biking around Alcoi is defined by natural parks, greenways, and river gorges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Alcoi are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 400 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Alcoi's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available in Alcoi?
A: Alcoi offers a wide selection of gravel bike trails, with over 30 routes available for exploration. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate loops to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails in Alcoi?
A: Gravel biking in Alcoi features diverse terrain, including mountainous backdrops, river gorges, and extensive greenways. You'll encounter Mediterranean mixed forests, especially within the natural parks, and routes that follow old railway tracks, offering a mix of surfaces and elevations.

Q: What natural parks can I explore while gravel biking in Alcoi?
A: A significant portion of Alcoi's territory is covered by the &lt;strong&gt;Font Roja Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Mariola Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1922606539/tour-of-the-font-roja-natural-park-loop-from-alcoy" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the Font Roja Natural Park: loop from Alcoy&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to experience the dense Mediterranean forest of Font Roja.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible by gravel bike in Alcoi?
A: Yes, Alcoi's routes often intertwine with historical and cultural sites. You can explore the region's industrial heritage, including the Molinar industrial complex. Additionally, nearby attractions include several castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3621301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castalla Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3097812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barchell Castle&lt;/a&gt;, as well as ancient ice houses (cavas) in the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4752696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Canyo Ice Well&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that feature Alcoi's famous bridges or greenways?
A: Absolutely. Alcoi is known as the 'City of Bridges,' and many routes offer spectacular views of the viaducts over the Riquer and Molinar rivers. The region also boasts several greenways, repurposed from former railway tracks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1922607503/serpis-greenway-and-beniarres-reservoir-loop-from-alcoy" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serpis Greenway and Beniarrés Reservoir: loop from Alcoy&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, following a historic greenway.

Q: Are there easy gravel bike trails suitable for beginners in Alcoi?
A: While many routes in Alcoi are rated as difficult, there are moderate options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39560846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Antoni Tunnel – L'Estepar Rest Area loop from Alcoi&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that offers varied terrain without extreme difficulty, making it suitable for those with good basic fitness.

Q: What options are there for experienced gravel bikers seeking a challenge?
A: Experienced gravel bikers will find plenty of challenging routes in Alcoi. The mountainous terrain offers demanding climbs, such as those found in the Font Roja Natural Park, which can be part of longer, difficult loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1922606539/tour-of-the-font-roja-natural-park-loop-from-alcoy" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the Font Roja Natural Park: loop from Alcoy&lt;/a&gt;, providing significant elevation gains and spectacular views.

Q: Are there circular gravel bike routes in Alcoi?
A: Yes, many of the gravel bike trails in Alcoi are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1922606539/tour-of-the-font-roja-natural-park-loop-from-alcoy" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the Font Roja Natural Park: loop from Alcoy&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1922607503/serpis-greenway-and-beniarres-reservoir-loop-from-alcoy" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serpis Greenway and Beniarrés Reservoir: loop from Alcoy&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other gravel bikers say about the trails in Alcoi?
A: The gravel biking routes in Alcoi are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. Many users praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural park scenery, and the well-maintained greenways that make for an enjoyable riding experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Alcoi?
A: Alcoi is an excellent destination for gravel biking throughout much of the year. Autumn is particularly beautiful in the Font Roja Natural Park, when ash and maple trees display spectacular colors. Spring also offers pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended, especially if tackling challenging climbs.

Q: Is public transport an option for accessing gravel trails in Alcoi?
A: Alcoi is well-connected within the Alicante Province, and while specific public transport options directly to trailheads might vary, the city itself serves as a good base. For detailed information on bus or train services that accommodate bicycles, it's best to check local transport schedules or visit the official Alcoi tourism website: &lt;a href="https://www.visitalcoy.com/en/naturaleza/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitalcoy.com&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What about parking facilities for gravel bikers in Alcoi?
A: Alcoi, as a city, offers various parking options. Many gravel routes start from or near urban areas, making it convenient to find parking. Specific trailheads within the natural parks or along greenways may also have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route for the most convenient parking information.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly gravel bike routes in Alcoi?
A: Many natural areas in Alcoi, including parts of the natural parks and greenways, are generally accessible for dogs, provided they are kept under control, often on a leash. However, specific regulations can vary within protected areas. Always check local signage and guidelines for the particular trail you plan to ride to ensure it's dog-friendly.

Q: Can I find family-friendly gravel bike trails in Alcoi?
A: Yes, Alcoi offers options suitable for families, particularly along its greenways. These repurposed railway tracks often provide flatter, well-maintained surfaces away from traffic, making them ideal for a more relaxed family outing. The 12 km Greenway of Alcoy, with its tunnels and viaducts, is a good example of a route that can be enjoyed by families.
tips: The greenway runs along the old railway line that linked Alcoy with Alicante and has now been refurbished for cyclists and hikers. Just over 8 km long and 90% paved, the route is very picturesque and entertaining. There are several tunnels to cross, some of which are several hundred metres long and are illuminated. An ideal route for the whole family.
This section from the slip road is not particularly developed, but was ok in the dry! Might take a bit or finding from the road but there is access.
Very fun path that runs through the ravine, it is a short section that then connects with a forest track but it is very entertaining for the mtb and does not present technical difficulty.
